devanagari: शौनक उवाच । महीं प्रतिष्ठामध्यस्य सौते स्वायम्भुवो मनुः । कानि अन्वतिष्ठद् द्वाराणि मार्गाय अवर जन्मनाम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śaunaka uvācamahīṃ pratiṣṭhāmadhyasya saute svāyambhuvo manuḥkāni anvatiṣṭhad dvārāṇi mārgāya avara janmanām
translation:
Śaunaka said:
Oh son of Sūta Romaharṣaṇa, having established himself on the earth, what did Svāyambhuva Manu do to create openings (ways) for the creation of beings of later birth (as they were absorbed within God)?

devanagari: क्षत्ता महाभागवतः कृष्णस्यैकान्तिकः सुहृत् । यस्तत्याजाग्रजं कृष्णे सापत्यं अघवानिति ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣattā mahābhāgavataḥ kṛṣṇasyaikāntikaḥ suhṛtyastatyājāgrajaṃ kṛṣṇe sāpatyaṃ aghavāniti
translation: The great devotee (of Kṛṣṇa) viz., Vidura, was absolutely devoted to Kṛṣṇa.[1] He abandoned his elder brother (Dhṛtarāṣtṛa) along with his sons, as he was wicked to Kṛṣṇa.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
aikāntikaḥ suhṛt: One who is convinced about Hari being the only supreme being.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: द्वैपायनादनवरो महित्वे तस्य देहजः । सर्वात्मना श्रितः कृष्णं तत्परांश्चाप्यनुव्रतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dvaipāyanādanavaro mahitve tasya dehajaḥsarvātmanā śritaḥ kṛṣṇaṃ tatparāṃścāpyanuvrataḥ
translation: Vidura who was born of Dvaipāyana, was in no way inferior to him in greatness. He was devoted to Kṛṣṇa with all his heart.[2] He was also attached to the devotees (of the Lord).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
sarvātmanā: (i) Thinking him to be his every relation such as father, mother etc.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: किं अन्वपृच्छन् मैत्रेयं विरजास्तीर्थसेवया । उपगम्य कुशावर्त आसीनं तत्त्ववित्तमम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ anvapṛcchan maitreyaṃ virajāstīrthasevayāupagamya kuśāvarta āsīnaṃ tattvavittamam
translation: Vidura cleansed his sins by resorting to sacred places. What did he ask of Maitreya who was the foremost one among the knowers of the truth after approaching him while he was sitting at Kuśāvarta?

devanagari: तयोः संवदतोः सूत प्रवृत्ता ह्यमलाः कथाः । आपो गाङ्गा इवाघघ्नीः हरेः पादाम्बुजाश्रयाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ saṃvadatoḥ sūta pravṛttā hyamalāḥ kathāḥāpo gāṅgā ivāghaghnīḥ hareḥ pādāmbujāśrayāḥ
translation: Oh Sūta, while they were conversing, sacred stories relating to the lotuslike feet of Hari, must have issued—stories which were sanctifying like the waters of the Gaṅgā which resorts to his lotuslike feet.

devanagari: ता नः कीर्तय भद्रं ते कीर्तन्योदारकर्मणः । रसज्ञः को नु तृप्येत हरिलीलामृतं पिबन् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tā naḥ kīrtaya bhadraṃ te kīrtanyodārakarmaṇaḥrasajñaḥ ko nu tṛpyeta harilīlāmṛtaṃ piban
translation: Please extol to us the deeds (of Hari) which are sublime and worth describing. God may bless you. But what man of taste will feel satiated while drinking the nectar in the form of Hari’s līlās?

devanagari: एवं उग्रश्रवाः पृष्ट ऋषिभिः नैमिषायनैः । भगवति अर्पिताध्यात्मः तान् आह श्रूयतामिति ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ ugraśravāḥ pṛṣṭa ṛṣibhiḥ naimiṣāyanaiḥbhagavati arpitādhyātmaḥ tān āha śrūyatāmiti
translation: Ugraśravas who was thus asked by the sages dwelling in the Naimiṣa forest fixed his mind on the Lord and spoke to them, ‘Please hear’.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । हरेर्धृतक्रोडतनोः स्वमायया । निशम्य गोरुद्धरणं रसातलात् । लीलां हिरण्याक्षमवज्ञया हतं । सञ्जातहर्षो मुनिमाह भारतः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācaharerdhṛtakroḍatanoḥ svamāyayāniśamya goruddharaṇaṃ rasātalātlīlāṃ hiraṇyākṣamavajñayā hataṃsañjātaharṣo munimāha bhārataḥ
translation:
Sūta said:
Having heard the lifting up of the earth from the Rasātala by Hari assuming the body of a boar through his Māyā, and Hari’s līlā of easily slaying Hiraṇyākṣa, Vidura felt rejoiced and asked the sage (Maitreya).

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । प्रजापतिपतिः सृष्ट्वा प्रजासर्गे प्रजापतीन् । किं आरभत मे ब्रह्मन् प्रब्रूह्यव्यक्तमार्गवित् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvācaprajāpatipatiḥ sṛṣṭvā prajāsarge prajāpatīnkiṃ ārabhata me brahman prabrūhyavyaktamārgavit
translation:
Vidura said:
Oh Brāhmaṇa, God Brahmā knows the course of the unmanifested Lord.[3] What did the Lord of Prajāpatis begin to do for the creation of beings after procreating Prajāpatis (progenitors like Marīci etc.)?
purport:
Footnote [3]:
avyakta-mārga-vit: Bhāvāratha Dīpikā takes it to qualify Maitreya and interprets: “who knows the reality which is not within the range of our senses.”

devanagari: ये मरीच्यादयो विप्रा यस्तु स्वायम्भुवो मनुः । ते वै ब्रह्मण आदेशात् कथं एतद् अभावयन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ye marīcyādayo viprā yastu svāyambhuvo manuḥte vai brahmaṇa ādeśāt kathaṃ etad abhāvayan
translation: How did Brāhmaṇas like Marīci and Svāyambhuva Manu create this world at the behest of Brahmā?

devanagari: सद्वितीयाः किमसृजन् स्वतन्त्रा उत कर्मसु । आहो स्वित्संहताः सर्व इदं स्म समकल्पयन् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sadvitīyāḥ kimasṛjan svatantrā uta karmasuāho svitsaṃhatāḥ sarva idaṃ sma samakalpayan
translation: Did they create this world with their wives? Or did they do so independently (without wives?) Or was this world produced jointly by them all?

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । दैवेन दुर्वितर्क्येण परेणानिमिषेण च । जातक्षोभाद् भगवतो महान् आसीद् गुणत्रयात् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācadaivena durvitarkyeṇa pareṇānimiṣeṇa cajātakṣobhād bhagavato mahān āsīd guṇatrayāt
translation:
Maitreya said:
The principle called Mahat was evolved out of Prakṛti composed of three guṇas (viz. Sattva, Rajas and Tamas) which got agitated by (the will of) the unperturbable Lord due to the incomprehensible destiny (adṛṣṭa) of jīvas, by the will power of the Supreme Being (the controller of Prakṛti) and through the force of unwinking (ever alert) Time.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
As usual, commentators impose their views of creation on this verse. For example, Padaratnāvalī: Due to the impelling of Lord Viṣṇu who lay on his bed along with Lakṣmī, on the waters of the deluvian ocean, there was disturbance in the balance of guṇas leading to the creation of Mahat and its presiding deity Brahmā. This agitation in guṇas was due to God who gave effect to the destiny (adṛṣṭa) of jīvas and the Time of creation.
Subodhinī states that Mahat did not evolve out of Prakṛti, but was due to the disturbance in the attributes of Brahman. The three attributes of Brahman are: (1) Daiva (Lord’s will, or desire to create), (2) Para i.e. Puruṣa or akṣara. This is Rājasa; (3) Kāla (the ever alert Time. This is Tāmasa). Daiva or Lord’s will being indescribable is called incomprehensible. The agitation of the three attributes (guṇas) of the Lord led to the evolution of Mahat.

devanagari: रजःप्रधानान् महतः त्रिलिङ्गो दैवचोदितात् । जातः ससर्ज भूतादिः वियदादीनि पञ्चशः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: rajaḥpradhānān mahataḥ triliṅgo daivacoditātjātaḥ sasarja bhūtādiḥ viyadādīni pañcaśaḥ
translation: Ahaṃkāra (bhūtādi) was created out of Mahat which was predominently full of rajas. It (ahaṃkāra) was of three forms (namely Vaikārika, Rājasa and Tāmasa) and it was urged by adṛṣṭa (destiny of jīvas). It (ahaṃkāra) created the groups of five each: of subtle elements (tanmātrās), five gross elements such as the sky etc., five conative organs of senses and five cognitive organs of senses and their presiding deities.

devanagari: तानि चैकैकशः स्रष्टुं असमर्थानि भौतिकम् । संहत्य दैवयोगेन हैमं अण्डं अवासृजन् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāni caikaikaśaḥ sraṣṭuṃ asamarthāni bhautikamsaṃhatya daivayogena haimaṃ aṇḍaṃ avāsṛjan
translation: The above things as separate ones were unable to create this Brahmāṇḍa of five bhūtas. When they came together by the inscrutable power of the Lord, they created the golden Egg.

devanagari: सोऽशयिष्टाब्धिसलिले आण्डकोशो निरात्मकः । साग्रं वै वर्षसाहस्रं अन्ववात्सीत् तं ईश्वरः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: so'śayiṣṭābdhisalile āṇḍakośo nirātmakaḥsāgraṃ vai varṣasāhasraṃ anvavātsīt taṃ īśvaraḥ
translation: That Egg being devoid of Ātman or Intelligent Being to preside over it, lay for one thousand years in the waters of the ocean. (At the end of that period) Īśvara (the controlling Almighty) entered into it.

devanagari: तस्य नाभेरभूत्पद्मं सहस्रार्कोरुदीधिति । सर्वजीवनिकायौको यत्र स्वयं अभूत्स्वराट् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya nābherabhūtpadmaṃ sahasrārkorudīdhitisarvajīvanikāyauko yatra svayaṃ abhūtsvarāṭ
translation: From his navel sprouted forth a lotus with the great splendour of one thousand sons. It was the abode of all living beings. Therein was manifested God Brahmā.

devanagari: सोऽनुविष्टो भगवता यः शेते सलिलाशये । लोकसंस्थां यथा पूर्वं निर्ममे संस्थया स्वया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: so'nuviṣṭo bhagavatā yaḥ śete salilāśayelokasaṃsthāṃ yathā pūrvaṃ nirmame saṃsthayā svayā
translation: God Brahmā who was guided by the Supreme Being who was lying on the ocean, created the well-arranged universe as before, with its own arrangement of names and forms.

devanagari: ससर्ज च्छाययाविद्यां पञ्चपर्वाणमग्रतः । तामिस्रं अन्धतामिस्रं तमो मोहो महातमः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sasarja cchāyayāvidyāṃ pañcaparvāṇamagrataḥtāmisraṃ andhatāmisraṃ tamo moho mahātamaḥ
translation: Out of his ignorance or Tamoguṇa, Brahmā created avidyā of five forms, viz., tāmisra, andha-tāmisra, tamas, moha, and mahātamas.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
For these avidyās vide supra III.10,17; III.12.2.

devanagari: विससर्जात्मनः कायं नाभिनन्दन् तमोमयम् । जगृहुर्यक्षरक्षांसि रात्रिं क्षुत्तृट्समुद्‍भवाम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: visasarjātmanaḥ kāyaṃ nābhinandan tamomayamjagṛhuryakṣarakṣāṃsi rātriṃ kṣuttṛṭsamud‍bhavām
translation: Not being pleased with his body which was composed of tamas, he cast it off. Yakṣas and Rākṣasas accepted that body which was in the form of night and which was the cause of hunger and thirst.

devanagari: क्षुत्तृड्भ्यां उपसृष्टास्ते तं जग्धुमभिदुद्रुवुः । मा रक्षतैनं जक्षध्वं इति ऊचुः क्षुत्तृडर्दिताः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛḍbhyāṃ upasṛṣṭāste taṃ jagdhumabhidudruvuḥmā rakṣatainaṃ jakṣadhvaṃ iti ūcuḥ kṣuttṛḍarditāḥ
translation: Being overcome with hunger and thirst, they ran at him to eat him up. Being afflicted with hunger and thirst they cried out, “Don’t protect him, devour him”.

devanagari: देवस्तानाह संविग्नो मा मां जक्षत रक्षत । अहो मे यक्षरक्षांसि प्रजा यूयं बभूविथ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: devastānāha saṃvigno mā māṃ jakṣata rakṣataaho me yakṣarakṣāṃsi prajā yūyaṃ babhūvitha
translation: God Brahmā being frightened, requested them, “Oh don’t devour me, protect me. You Yakṣas and Rākṣasas are born of me”.

devanagari: देवताः प्रभया या या दीव्यन् प्रमुखतोऽसृजत् । ते अहार्षुर्देवयन्तो विसृष्टां तां प्रभामहः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: devatāḥ prabhayā yā yā dīvyan pramukhato'sṛjatte ahārṣurdevayanto visṛṣṭāṃ tāṃ prabhāmahaḥ
translation: Shining with brilliant light he chiefly produced these deities who playing with the light, i.e. day time, thus shed by him, claimed the day as theirs.

devanagari: देवोऽदेवाञ्जघनतः सृजति स्मातिलोलुपान् । ते एनं लोलुपतया मैथुनायाभिपेदिरे ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: devo'devāñjaghanataḥ sṛjati smātilolupānte enaṃ lolupatayā maithunāyābhipedire
translation: God Brahmā created sexually over-passionate Asuras from the lower part of his body. Out of lust, they approached him for copulation.

devanagari: ततो हसन् स भगवान् असुरैर्निरपत्रपैः । अन्वीयमानस्तरसा क्रुद्धो भीतः परापतत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tato hasan sa bhagavān asurairnirapatrapaiḥanvīyamānastarasā kruddho bhītaḥ parāpatat
translation: Thereupon god Brahmā laughed. But when he was pursued by the shameless Asuras, he got enraged, afraid and fled away in great haste.

devanagari: स उपव्रज्य वरदं प्रपन्नार्तिहरं हरिम् । अनुग्रहाय भक्तानां अनुरूपात्मदर्शनम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa upavrajya varadaṃ prapannārtiharaṃ harimanugrahāya bhaktānāṃ anurūpātmadarśanam
translation: He approached Hari who removes the distress of the afflicted and confers boons on them, and who with a view to bestowing his grace, manifests himself to his devotees in the form desired by them.

devanagari: पाहि मां परमात्मंस्ते प्रेषणेनासृजं प्रजाः । ता इमा यभितुं पापा उपाक्रामन्ति मां प्रभो ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: pāhi māṃ paramātmaṃste preṣaṇenāsṛjaṃ prajāḥtā imā yabhituṃ pāpā upākrāmanti māṃ prabho
translation: (Brahmā prayed): ‘Oh Supreme Soul, protect me. It is at your command that I created (these) beings. Oh Lord, these wicked beings fall upon me to satiate their lust.

devanagari: त्वमेकः किल लोकानां क्लिष्टानां क्लेशनाशनः । त्वमेकः क्लेशदस्तेषां अनासन्न पदां तव ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamekaḥ kila lokānāṃ kliṣṭānāṃ kleśanāśanaḥtvamekaḥ kleśadasteṣāṃ anāsanna padāṃ tava
translation: You are the only one who can certainly remove the distress of the afflicted persons. You are the only one who can give trouble to those who do not resort to your feet’.

devanagari: सोऽवधार्यास्य कार्पण्यं विविक्ताध्यात्मदर्शनः । विमुञ्चात्मतनुं घोरां इत्युक्तो विमुमोच ह ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: so'vadhāryāsya kārpaṇyaṃ viviktādhyātmadarśanaḥvimuñcātmatanuṃ ghorāṃ ityukto vimumoca ha
translation: (Hari) who vividly reads the minds of others understood Brahmā’s pitiable condition and told him, “Give up your terrible body”. Being thus commanded, Brahmā abandoned it.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that all such references to abandon the body mean eschewing the particular state of mind and assumption of body implies assumption of a particular state of mind.

devanagari: तां क्वणच्चरणाम्भोजां मदविह्वल लोचनाम् । काञ्चीकलापविलसद् दुकूलत् छन्न रोधसम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ kvaṇaccaraṇāmbhojāṃ madavihvala locanāmkāñcīkalāpavilasad dukūlat channa rodhasam
translation: [7] Oh Vidura, all the Asuras thought (the evening to be a woman) and were foolishly infatuated of her—a woman whose lotuslike feet were jingling with anklets, whose eyes were overcome with intoxication, whose loins (middle) were covered with shining silken sāri (fixed up) with a girdle of small tinkling bells, whose big high breasts closely brushing each other left no space on her bosom (i.e. completely occupied it), whose nose was shapely, rows of teeth beautiful, smiles fascinating and glances sportive. The mass of hair (on whose head) were dark blue. She (as it were) concealed herself (from their glances) out of shyness.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Bhāgavata Candrikā state that the body thus given up by Brahmā became the evening time when sexual passions are normally aroused. This explanation introduces the following description of the evening as a beautiful lady.

devanagari: अन्योन्यश्लेषयोत्तुङ्ग निरन्तरपयोधराम् । सुनासां सुद्विजां स्निग्ध हासलीलावलोकनाम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: anyonyaśleṣayottuṅga nirantarapayodharāmsunāsāṃ sudvijāṃ snigdha hāsalīlāvalokanām
translation: [7] Oh Vidura, all the Asuras thought (the evening to be a woman) and were foolishly infatuated of her—a woman whose lotuslike feet were jingling with anklets, whose eyes were overcome with intoxication, whose loins (middle) were covered with shining silken sāri (fixed up) with a girdle of small tinkling bells, whose big high breasts closely brushing each other left no space on her bosom (i.e. completely occupied it), whose nose was shapely, rows of teeth beautiful, smiles fascinating and glances sportive. The mass of hair (on whose head) were dark blue. She (as it were) concealed herself (from their glances) out of shyness.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Bhāgavata Candrikā state that the body thus given up by Brahmā became the evening time when sexual passions are normally aroused. This explanation introduces the following description of the evening as a beautiful lady.

devanagari: गूहन्तीं व्रीडयात्मानं नीलालकवरूथिनीम् । उपलभ्यासुरा धर्म सर्वे सम्मुमुहुः स्त्रियम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: gūhantīṃ vrīḍayātmānaṃ nīlālakavarūthinīmupalabhyāsurā dharma sarve sammumuhuḥ striyam
translation: [7] Oh Vidura, all the Asuras thought (the evening to be a woman) and were foolishly infatuated of her—a woman whose lotuslike feet were jingling with anklets, whose eyes were overcome with intoxication, whose loins (middle) were covered with shining silken sāri (fixed up) with a girdle of small tinkling bells, whose big high breasts closely brushing each other left no space on her bosom (i.e. completely occupied it), whose nose was shapely, rows of teeth beautiful, smiles fascinating and glances sportive. The mass of hair (on whose head) were dark blue. She (as it were) concealed herself (from their glances) out of shyness.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Bhāgavata Candrikā state that the body thus given up by Brahmā became the evening time when sexual passions are normally aroused. This explanation introduces the following description of the evening as a beautiful lady.

devanagari: अहो रूपमहो धैर्यं अहो अस्या नवं वयः । मध्ये कामयमानानां अकामेव विसर्पति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: aho rūpamaho dhairyaṃ aho asyā navaṃ vayaḥmadhye kāmayamānānāṃ akāmeva visarpati
translation: (The Asuras appreciated in wonder): “Oh what a beautiful form! What boldness! What a fascinating prime of youth! Without a touch of passion she moves amongst us who are full of lust.”

devanagari: वितर्कयन्तो बहुधा तां सन्ध्यां प्रमदाकृतिम् । अभिसम्भाव्य विश्रम्भात् पर्यपृच्छन् कुमेधसः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vitarkayanto bahudhā tāṃ sandhyāṃ pramadākṛtimabhisambhāvya viśrambhāt paryapṛcchan kumedhasaḥ
translation: Entertaining different ideas about the Evening in the form of a young woman, the wicked-minded Asuras, out of lust, asked her courteously:

devanagari: कासि कस्यासि रम्भोरु को वार्थस्तेऽत्र भामिनि । रूपद्रविणपण्येन दुर्भगान्नो विबाधसे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kāsi kasyāsi rambhoru ko vārthaste'tra bhāminirūpadraviṇapaṇyena durbhagānno vibādhase
translation: “Oh lady of beautiful thighs, who are you? Whose daughter are you? Oh beautiful girl, what is your object here? Why do you torment us, the unfortunate ones by exhibiting the invaluable commodity of your beauty?

devanagari: या वा काचित्त्वमबले दिष्ट्या सन्दर्शनं तव । उत्सुनोषीक्षमाणानां कन्दुकक्रीडया मनः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yā vā kācittvamabale diṣṭyā sandarśanaṃ tavautsunoṣīkṣamāṇānāṃ kandukakrīḍayā manaḥ
translation: You may be anybody. But we have the good fortune of having a look at you. You agitate the hearts of the onlookers by your play with the ball.

devanagari: नैकत्र ते जयति शालिनि पादपद्मं । घ्नन्त्या मुहुः करतलेन पतत्पतङ्गम् । मध्यं विषीदति बृहत्स्तनभारभीतं । शान्तेव दृष्टिरमला सुशिखासमूहः ॥...
english_devnagari: naikatra te jayati śālini pādapadmaṃghnantyā muhuḥ karatalena patatpataṅgammadhyaṃ viṣīdati bṛhatstanabhārabhītaṃśānteva dṛṣṭiramalā suśikhāsamūhaḥ
translation: Oh beautiful lady, while you are frequently beating down the falling ball with the palm of your hand, your lotuslike feet are not steady at any place. Your middle (waist) being afraid of the heavy burden of your big breasts, feels fatigued. Your clear eyes appear serene[8] and the braided hair beautiful.”

devanagari: इति सायन्तनीं सन्ध्यां असुराः प्रमदायतीम् । प्रलोभयन्तीं जगृहुः मत्वा मूढधियः स्त्रियम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sāyantanīṃ sandhyāṃ asurāḥ pramadāyatīmpralobhayantīṃ jagṛhuḥ matvā mūḍhadhiyaḥ striyam
translation: In this way, the dull-witted Asuras, thinking the evening time to be a woman as it appeared to them as such, and attracted their hearts, accepted her.

devanagari: प्रहस्य भावगम्भीरं जिघ्रन्ति आत्मानमात्मना । कान्त्या ससर्ज भगवान् गन्धर्वाप्सरसां गणान् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: prahasya bhāvagambhīraṃ jighranti ātmānamātmanākāntyā sasarja bhagavān gandharvāpsarasāṃ gaṇān
translation: With a laugh of deep erotic significance god Brahmā created the tribes called Gandharvas (heavenly musicians) and Apsarās (celestial damsels) by his self-appreciating personal charm.

devanagari: विससर्ज तनुं तां वै ज्योत्स्नां कान्तिमतीं प्रियाम् । ते एव चाददुः प्रीत्या विश्वावसुपुरोगमाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: visasarja tanuṃ tāṃ vai jyotsnāṃ kāntimatīṃ priyāmte eva cādaduḥ prītyā viśvāvasupurogamāḥ
translation: He verily gave up that splendid lovable body of moonlight which Gandharvas headed by Viśvāvasu accepted with joy.

devanagari: सृष्ट्वा भूतपिशाचांश्च भगवान् आत्मतन्द्रिणा । दिग्वाससो मुक्तकेशान् वीक्ष्य चामीलयद् दृशौ ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: sṛṣṭvā bhūtapiśācāṃśca bhagavān ātmatandriṇādigvāsaso muktakeśān vīkṣya cāmīlayad dṛśau
translation: Creating ghosts and goblins out of his lassitude, he closed his eyes when he saw their nudity and dishevelled hair.

devanagari: जगृहुस्तद्विसृष्टां तां जृम्भणाख्यां तनुं प्रभोः । निद्रां इन्द्रियविक्लेदो यया भूतेषु दृश्यते । येनोच्छिष्टान्धर्षयन्ति तमुन्मादं प्रच...
english_devnagari: jagṛhustadvisṛṣṭāṃ tāṃ jṛmbhaṇākhyāṃ tanuṃ prabhoḥnidrāṃ indriyavikledo yayā bhūteṣu dṛśyateyenocchiṣṭāndharṣayanti tamunmādaṃ pracakṣate
translation: They took over god Brahmā’s body called ‘yawning’[9] which was abandoned by him. By that body is created that complete sluggishness of senses called ‘sleep’ among living beings. They (goblins) possess the impure beings in that stage (of relaxation). It is called madness (unmāda).
purport:
Footnote [9]:
The bodies of bhūtas are fourfold: (1)Tandrā (lassitude), (2) Jṛmbhā (yawning), (3) Nidrā (sleep), (4) Unmāda (madness) Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: ऊर्जस्वन्तं मन्यमान आत्मानं भगवानजः । साध्यान् गणान् पितृगणान् परोक्षेणासृजत्प्रभुः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: ūrjasvantaṃ manyamāna ātmānaṃ bhagavānajaḥsādhyān gaṇān pitṛgaṇān parokṣeṇāsṛjatprabhuḥ
translation: Feeling himself possessing procreative power, venerable Brahmā created the class of celestial beings called Sādhyas and Pitṛs (manes) while he kept himself invisible.

devanagari: ते आत्मसर्गं तं कायं पितरः प्रतिपेदिरे । साध्येभ्यश्च पितृभ्यश्च कवयो यद्वितन्वते ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: te ātmasargaṃ taṃ kāyaṃ pitaraḥ pratipediresādhyebhyaśca pitṛbhyaśca kavayo yadvitanvate
translation: Pitṛs took possession of the body from which they were created and on account of that, experts in Karma-path give to Sādhyas and manes (the offerings called havya and kavya due to them).

devanagari: सिद्धान् विद्याधरांश्चैव तिरोधानेन सोऽसृजत् । तेभ्योऽददात् तं आत्मानं अन्तर्धानाख्यमद्‍भुतम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhān vidyādharāṃścaiva tirodhānena so'sṛjattebhyo'dadāt taṃ ātmānaṃ antardhānākhyamad‍bhutam
translation: By his power of remaining invisible, he created Siddhas and Vidyādharas (demigods) and passed on to them his miraculous body called antardhāna (disappearance).

devanagari: स किन्नरान् किम्पुरुषान् प्रत्यात्म्येनासृजत्प्रभुः । मानयन्नात्मनात्मानं आत्माभासं विलोकयन् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa kinnarān kimpuruṣān pratyātmyenāsṛjatprabhuḥmānayannātmanātmānaṃ ātmābhāsaṃ vilokayan
translation: Decorating his body (with sandal-paste, flowers etc.) and appreciating his reflection (in the mirror) Brahmā created from his image Kinnaras and Kiṃpuruṣas.

devanagari: ते तु तज्जगृहू रूपं त्यक्तं यत्परमेष्ठिना । मिथुनीभूय गायन्तः तं एवोषसि कर्मभिः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: te tu tajjagṛhū rūpaṃ tyaktaṃ yatparameṣṭhināmithunībhūya gāyantaḥ taṃ evoṣasi karmabhiḥ
translation: They accepted the form (characterised by Narcissism) abandoned by Brahmā. Hence they get together with their wives at dawn and sing in praise of his deeds.

devanagari: देहेन वै भोगवता शयानो बहुचिन्तया । सर्गेऽनुपचिते क्रोधात् उत्ससर्ज ह तद्वपुः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: dehena vai bhogavatā śayāno bahucintayāsarge'nupacite krodhāt utsasarja ha tadvapuḥ
translation: Lying with the extremities of his body fully extended and full of deep anxiety at the insufficient growth of the creation, he angrily cast off that body (which became characterised by anger, extension etc.).

devanagari: येऽहीयन्तामुतः केशा अहयस्तेऽङ्ग जज्ञिरे । सर्पाः प्रसर्पतः क्रूरा नागा भोगोरुकन्धराः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: ye'hīyantāmutaḥ keśā ahayaste'ṅga jajñiresarpāḥ prasarpataḥ krūrā nāgā bhogorukandharāḥ
translation: Oh Vidura, the hair dropped from that body became serpents. From the body which was moving here and there were born cruel cobras with big hoods and broad necks.

devanagari: स आत्मानं मन्यमानः कृतकृत्यमिवात्मभूः । तदा मनून् ससर्जान्ते मनसा लोकभावनान् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ātmānaṃ manyamānaḥ kṛtakṛtyamivātmabhūḥtadā manūn sasarjānte manasā lokabhāvanān
translation: When the self-born god (Brahmā) felt that he had achieved his purpose, he created from his mind Manus, progenitors of prolific population on the world.

devanagari: तेभ्यः सोऽसृजत्स्वीयं पुरं पुरुषमात्मवान् । तान् दृष्ट्वा ये पुरा सृष्टाः प्रशशंसुः प्रजापतिम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyaḥ so'sṛjatsvīyaṃ puraṃ puruṣamātmavāntān dṛṣṭvā ye purā sṛṣṭāḥ praśaśaṃsuḥ prajāpatim
translation: The self-possessed Brahmā gave up his body in human form to the Manus. Seeing the Manus, Beings who were previously created highly praised the Lord of Prajās (Brahmā).

devanagari: अहो एतत् जगत्स्रष्टः सुकृतं बत ते कृतम् । प्रतिष्ठिताः क्रिया यस्मिन् साकं अन्नमदाम हे ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: aho etat jagatsraṣṭaḥ sukṛtaṃ bata te kṛtampratiṣṭhitāḥ kriyā yasmin sākaṃ annamadāma he
translation: “Oh creator of the world, what you have done is verily well-done. In this (Manu-creation) all the religious courses are well-established. (Herein) all of us eat food together.”

devanagari: तपसा विद्यया युक्तो योगेन सुसमाधिना । ऋषीन् ऋषिः हृषीकेशः ससर्जाभिमताः प्रजाः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: tapasā vidyayā yukto yogena susamādhināṛṣīn ṛṣiḥ hṛṣīkeśaḥ sasarjābhimatāḥ prajāḥ
translation: The sage Brahmā who possesses (the power of) penance, knowledge, devotion, Yoga along with the power of profound meditation and perfect control over sense-organs, created the sage-world (ṛṣi-sarga) so dear to him.

devanagari: तेभ्यश्चैकैकशः स्वस्य देहस्यांशमदादजः । यत्तत् समाधियोगर्द्धि तपोविद्याविरक्तिमत् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyaścaikaikaśaḥ svasya dehasyāṃśamadādajaḥyattat samādhiyogarddhi tapovidyāviraktimat
translation:
To each one of the sages, god Brahmā gave a portion of his body characterised by complete meditation, yoga, miraculous powers, penance, knowledge and non-attachment.
[back to top]
